title: gerascanthus aurantiacus
intro: Gerascanthus aurantiacus, also known as the 'Orange-flowered Gerascanthus', is a flowering plant species in the family Apocynaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including the relief of fever, pain, and respiratory issues, as well as the promotion of wound healing and the treatment of skin conditions. The herbal preparations of Gerascanthus aurantiacus typically involve the leaves and flowers being used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are then consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
